intended_use: Oncospace is used to configure and review radiotherapy treatment plans for a patient with malignant or benign disease in the head and neck, thoracic, abdominal, and pelvic regions. It allows for set u
intended_use_full: Oncospace is used to configure and review radiotherapy treatment plans for a patient with malignant or benign disease in the head and neck, thoracic, abdominal, and pelvic regions. It allows for set up of radiotherapy treatment protocols, association of a potential treatment plan with the protocol(s), submission of a dose prescription and achievable dosimetric goals to a treatment planning system, and review of the treatment plan. It is intended for use by qualified, trained radiation therapy professionals (such as medical physicists, oncologists, and dosimetrists). This device is for prescription use by order of a physician.
target_condition: malignant or benign disease in the head and neck, thoracic, abdominal, and pelvic regions requiring radiotherapy
ground_truth: Curated, gold-standard treatment plans; retrospective clinical treatment plans; prospective clinical treatment plans from 2021-2024
other_metrics: Model performance: External validation mean absolute error in OAR DVH dose values within 5% of prescription dose for majority of OARs (9/12 for Institution_2 HN, 10/12 for Institution_3 HN, 4/6 for Institution_3 prostate). NLP model achieved macro-averaged F1 score above 0.92 and accuracy above 96% in 5-fold cross-validation; external testing correctly matched 207/221 (94.1%) of structure names. Clinical performance: Non-inferiority demonstrated with mean OAR dose non-inferiority margins of 2.2 Gy for thoracic, 1 Gy for abdominal, 1.9 Gy for pelvis (gynecological). No statistically significant difference in target coverage between clinical plans and Oncospace-assisted plans.
